title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: Paramount and Warner Bros merger on hold
slides.explanation: A judge has temporarily paused the massive $110 billion deal between these two media giants. It comes after state attorneys general raised concerns about the new company's market dominance.

slides.headline: EU slaps record fine on AliExpress
slides.explanation: The European Union has fined the retailer 550 million euros. Officials say the platform failed to stop the sale of unsafe toys and counterfeit clothing.

slides.headline: Blending old skills with new tech
slides.explanation: Companies are looking for new ways to train young workers. They are finding success by balancing modern technology skills with traditional, hands-on trades.

slides.headline: Could Apple stop OpenAI's hardware plans?
slides.explanation: There is a debate about whether a new lawsuit from Apple will disrupt OpenAI's future projects. People are watching to see if this affects their plans to build hardware.

slides.headline: Antitrust ruling blocks massive media deal
slides.explanation: A judge issued a restraining order to stop the Paramount and Warner Bros purchase for now. The court noted that the merger would likely break antitrust laws.

caption: From major media mergers being paused to record-breaking fines in Europe, here is your tech news roundup for today. We are breaking down the biggest headlines you need to know. Swipe through to catch up on the latest developments. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: Paramount and Warner Bros merger paused
slides.explanation: A US court has temporarily blocked the merger between Paramount and Warner Bros. This follows legal action taken by 12 states.

slides.headline: US strikes on Iran continue
slides.explanation: The US has launched new strikes against Iran. The goal is to stop Iran from attacking ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

slides.headline: Andy Burnham reveals new cabinet
slides.explanation: The new Prime Minister has announced his cabinet and appointed John Healey as chancellor. He promised a prudent approach to the economy while trying to bring hope back to the country.

slides.headline: Democrats push to stop Iran conflict
slides.explanation: After three US soldiers died in Iranian strikes, Democrats are calling on President Trump to stop the war. Many are concerned about the rising tension and the loss of control.

slides.headline: Tensions rise as US hits Iran again
slides.explanation: The US military carried out its 10th round of strikes to try and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Iran has described the situation as a full-scale war.

caption: From major legal hurdles in the corporate world to escalating tensions in the Middle East, here is what you need to know today. We are tracking the latest developments in politics and global security so you can stay informed. Swipe through for the full breakdown. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
